---
title: "Sample Quarto Document"
author: "Test Author"
date: "2026-06-08"
format: html
---

## Introduction

This is a sample Quarto document for testing the pandoc word count extension.
It contains a mix of prose, formatting, and code to exercise different parts
of the visual editor.

You can **select any portion** of this text to see the selection word count
appear in the status bar alongside the total count.

## Methods

We used a variety of approaches to investigate the problem. The main steps were:

1. First, we collected data from multiple sources.
2. Then, we cleaned and normalised the data.
3. Finally, we ran our analysis pipeline.

The pipeline itself consists of a short R script shown below.

```{r}
#| echo: true
x <- c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
mean(x)
```

## Results

The results were largely as expected. The mean value was 3, which is consistent
with a uniform distribution over the integers 1 through 5.

> "Statistics is the grammar of science." — Karl Pearson

Here is a small summary table:

| Group | N  | Mean | SD   |
|-------|----|------|------|
| A     | 20 | 3.1  | 0.8  |
| B     | 18 | 2.9  | 1.1  |
| C     | 22 | 3.4  | 0.6  |

## Discussion

These findings suggest that the approach is broadly applicable. Further work
should focus on edge cases and on improving the robustness of the pipeline
under real-world conditions, where data quality is often lower than in
controlled experiments.

### Limitations

- Small sample sizes in some groups
- Potential confounding from external variables
- Results may not generalise beyond the current dataset

## Conclusion

In summary, this document demonstrates the core features of Quarto's visual
editor, including headings, prose, lists, tables, code blocks, and block
quotes. Select any of this text to test the word count extension.
